2606.23419.title: GRINQH: Graded Input-based Quantization Hierarchy for Efficient LLM Generation
2606.23419.paper_summary: Autoregressive decoding with LLMs is primarily bottlenecked by GPU memory bandwidth, especially in edge-computing settings. While quantization is essential for mitigating this bottleneck, most existing methods treat inference as a uniform process and fail to account for the asymmetry between the compute-bound prefill stage and the memory-bound decoding stage. We propose GRINQH (GRaded INput-based Quantization Hierarchy), a weight-only post-training quantization framework that accelerates decoding by unifying quantization and sparsification. GRINQH leverages activation magnitudes as a proxy for computational importance to dynamically assign weight channels to different precision levels, enabling flexible average bit widths during decoding. Evaluated on Llama3 and Qwen3 models, GRINQH outperforms state-of-the-art fixed- and mixed-precision baselines at comparable 3- and 4-bit settings, even enabling effective 2-bit generation. We experimentally verify theoretical speedups by leveraging a hierarchical nested memory layout for multi-precision storage in a custom GPU kernel. Ultimately, GRINQH establishes a new state-of-the-art Pareto frontier for LLM generation, enabling a dynamic trade-off between generation quality and inference speed.
2606.23419.paper_summary_zh: 自回歸解碼與 LLM 的主要瓶頸在於 GPU 記憶體帶寬，尤其是在邊緣計算環境中。雖然量化對於緩解這一瓶頸至關重要，但大多數現有方法將推理視為一個統一的過程，未能考慮到計算密集的預填充階段與記憶體密集的解碼階段之間的非對稱性。我們提出了 GRINQH（基於分級輸入的量化層級），這是一個僅基於權重的後訓練量化框架，通過統一量化和稀疏化來加速解碼。GRINQH 利用激活幅度作為計算重要性的代理，動態地將權重通道分配到不同的精度級別，從而在解碼過程中實現靈活的平均位寬。在 Llama3 和 Qwen3 模型上的評估中，GRINQH 在可比的 3 位和 4 位設置下超越了最先進的固定和混合精度基準，甚至實現了有效的 2 位生成。我們通過利用自定義 GPU 核心中的層級嵌套記憶體佈局來實驗驗證理論上的加速。最終，GRINQH 為 LLM 生成建立了一個新的最先進的 Pareto 邊界，實現了生成質量與推理速度之間的動態權衡。
2606.23419.author: Jette Oberländer et.al.
2606.23419.authors: Jette Oberländer, Jan Finkbeiner, Catherine M. Schöfmann, Emre Neftci
